failure_signature: AGVs stopped with tipper heartbeat timeout and no RMS alarm
symptom_summary: AGVs stopped, no active RMS alarms, all tippers reported heartbeat timeout, hospital tote removal hung, and the system appeared active but frozen.
root_cause_summary: Suspected WCS or Ignition service failure affecting heartbeat communication.
resolution_summary: Engineer restarted the Optisweep/Ignition service and validated heartbeat recovery before AGVs resumed movement.
resolution_steps: Confirm no RMS alarm is active.
Confirm all tippers show heartbeat timeout.
Place master E-stop ON before service recovery.
Validate heartbeat recovery and AGV movement.
escalation_notes: Escalate when heartbeat does not recover, remote access is unavailable, or OT hardware alarms are present.
source_notes: Curated from recurring Phase 0 CAT-1 signature evidence.
notes: Knowledge record provides evidence only; operational steps are executed from workflow YAML.

failure_signature: Heartbeat timeout without RMS alarm during hospital tote removal
symptom_summary: Hospital tote removal hung while AGVs were stopped and tipper heartbeat timeout was present without an RMS alarm.
root_cause_summary: Application service instability was suspected because the system looked active but operational flow was frozen.
resolution_summary: Service recovery required engineer involvement and post-restart heartbeat validation.
resolution_steps: Confirm heartbeat and AGV movement recovery.
escalation_notes: Escalate to application engineering when service restart is required.
source_notes: Curated from Phase 0 case 229716 candidate evidence.

failure_signature: Repeated frozen WCS presentation with heartbeat timeout
symptom_summary: System appeared active but frozen, AGVs were not moving, and heartbeat timeout symptoms matched the recurring CAT-1 pattern.
resolution_summary: Engineer-led service recovery and operational validation were used.
resolution_steps: Validate heartbeat and AGV movement after restart.
escalation_notes: Escalate if OT hardware alarms are present or heartbeat does not recover.
source_notes: Curated from related Phase 0 recurrence pattern.

failure_signature: UPS Haslet AGVs stopped moving; Optisweep service restored operations after restart
symptom_summary: Case 00229374 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that AGVs were not moving and nothing was feeding the tippers during preload. Site reported robots appeared to have tasks and totes but stopped before tipper lines, could not remove product at the hospital area, and RMS showed no robot errors. Remote troubleshooting was delayed by UPS-side Zscaler/VPN access issues. Site-side checks included operator station status, AVEVA review, pause/unpause attempts, and heartbeat checks, with Tipper 1 reported at 0 while other tippers had nonzero heartbeat values. Once access to 10.27.80.165 was obtained through a field PC, support observed Ignition up, reviewed database timeout and OPC/heartbeat-related evidence, and Mitchel Flynn restarted Optisweep services. The site continued to run after the restart. Root cause was not explicitly validated in source evidence.
source_notes: Case 00229374 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that AGVs were not moving and nothing was feeding the tippers during preload. Once access to 10.27.80.165 was obtained through a field PC, support observed Ignition up, reviewed database timeout and OPC/heartbeat-related evidence, and Mitchel Flynn restarted Optisweep services. The site continued to run after the restart. Root cause was not explicitly validated in source evidence.

failure_signature: UPS TXRTH WCS/service failure with AGVs not moving, tote removal blocked, and suspected Ignition/OptiSweep service disruption
symptom_summary: Case 228086 candidate CAT-1 WCS/service failure at UPS TXRTH on 2026-02-20. Reported symptoms included 4-6 AGVs not moving in various areas after a heartbeat issue, inability to remove totes from hospital, question marks on the HMI, and suspected Ignition crash or OptiSweep service disruption. Troubleshooting included remote access, review of Ignition memory trend and uptime/performance pages, RMS and AGV status review, GetAgvStatuses API testing, and later restart-oriented guidance for Ignition and OptiSweep validation. Evidence does not validate root cause. Escalation and customer communication gaps were also documented, especially lack of a proper UPS bridge call.
source_notes: Case 228086 candidate CAT-1 WCS/service failure at UPS TXRTH on 2026-02-20. failure_signature: UPS Fort Worth Haslet ACB hospital station unable to induct totes with AGV/tote state mismatch and recovery actions required
symptom_summary: Case 00223554 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) involved the ACB hospital station being unable to induct totes. Source records show the case opened around 2:11-2:12 AM on 2025-09-26, a CBRE work order was entered at 1:35 AM CST, and a Teams bridge was posted at 3:05 AM. Salesforce updates state the original issue was resolved by 4:07 AM with the site able to add and remove totes, and all robots were re-added by 5:05 AM. Later Teams discussion on the evening of 2025-09-26 describes a related or recurring hospital/tipper disruption where totes could be removed but not added, AGVs 1542188 and 1555028 were removed, AGV 1555012 had a disconnected tote 51, and API dog/Aveva endpoints were used to induce tote movement and remove a stuck tote. Multiple messages suggest a candidate cause involving an AGV being removed without removing its associated tote, but root cause is not explicitly validated in the source.

failure_signature: UPS Fort Worth (Haslet) AGV/tipper flow stoppage with heartbeat timeout indications; service restart restored operation
symptom_summary: Case 00229716 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that after startup everything was waiting, no path was showing, and nothing was coming to the tippers. During sort start, AGVs lined up to go to tippers and all three lines stopped. Operators could not remove a tote at the hospital. Tippers showed heartbeat timeout while remaining active and enabled, with no RMS faults noted. L2 remotely investigated, coordinated escalation in Teams and Salesforce, and under direction from Mitchel Flynn the team E-stopped the AGVs, restarted the Optisweep service, removed the E-stop, and may have paused/unpaused tippers. Antonio confirmed proper AGV operation and hospital function afterward. Ignition and Windows event logs were saved, and switch log follow-up was assigned. Root cause was not explicitly validated in the source.
source_notes: Case 00229716 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that after startup everything was waiting, no path was showing, and nothing was coming to the tippers. failure_signature: UPS Haslet AGV movement stoppage with repeated station/OPC UA disconnect behavior; service restart restored production
symptom_summary: Case 00229488 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) documents a repeat production-impacting incident where AGVs were not moving and operations referenced the same issue from Saturday case 00229374 / JIRA CSPO-9007. Early triage noted most tippers in run position except two, a heartbeat reported as flat at 550, and operators manually clearing areas to prevent service failures. The case was escalated to software support and internal escalation resources. Customer pressure emphasized restoring operations within 30 minutes while also obtaining root cause and timeline details. At approximately 9:27 AM on 4/8/2026, the Optisweep Windows service was restarted remotely, after which the site was reported operating and getting production. Later investigation referenced Ignition and visual alarm evidence showing hospital lane and GLD1/OPC UA connection resets, including 4/7 21:15 events with RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink module communication loss, VFD not detected alarms, and OPC UA connection reset behavior. Additional 4/8 and 4/9 evidence showed connection resets on 10.27.80.156 and 10.27.80.157, keep-alive failures, subscription transfer failures, reconnect/rebuild activity, and discussion of possible network degradation. Root cause was not validated in the source.

failure_signature: UPS Haslet OptiSweep/AGV startup stoppage with no paths to tippers; tipper heartbeat failure observed and service restart restored flow
symptom_summary: Case 00229777 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) describes an OptiSweep/AGV system startup issue where everything was waiting, no path was shown, and nothing was coming up to the tippers. Teams chat logged OptiSweep stopping around 5:25 AM on 2026-04-18. Kieran Hardy connected to the system, observed that the tipper heartbeat had failed similar to case 229716, and recovery actions included client placing the master e-stop on while OptiSweep was restarted and the tippers were paused and unpaused. The system was reported back up and running and the client agreed the case could be closed. Later Teams discussion on 2026-04-29 referenced network troubleshooting, disconnect logging, AGV-side logs, RMS/Athena logs, and a robot with frequent disconnect history, but these appear to be follow-on diagnostic context rather than the original outage event.
